LearnHubEditorial study atlas
Local-first
Light Mode
Topic
Elements
A deeper chemistry reading sequence on the first 118 elements, structured as compact academic mini-textbooks.
1 H
2 He
3 Li
4 Be
5 B
6 C
7 N
8 O
9 F
10 Ne
Collection
Elements library
Each unit opens as a small scientific book with chapter reading, glossary context, flashcards, quizzes, bookmarks, and local progress.
Start Here
1 H • Hydrogen
Start here to follow the intended sequence for this topic. Hydrogen is interpreted first through periodic placement, and this chapter explains how period 1, s-block, conventionally placed above Group 1 without behaving like a true alkali metal establishes the element's identity.
Atomic Identity and Periodic Classification
Topic sequence
1 H
Hydrogen
1
Hydrogen is the lightest element and a chemically unusual nonmetal whose single electron links acid-base chemistry, redox chemistry, isotopes, and energy systems.
1s1 electron configuration
Lowest-density element
Most abundant element in the universe
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
2 He
Helium
2
Helium is a closed-shell noble gas whose extraordinary physical properties make it far more important in cryogenics and measurement than its chemical reactivity would suggest.
Filled 1s shell
Lowest boiling point of any element
Second most abundant element in the universe
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
3 Li
Lithium
3
Lithium is the lightest metal, an alkali element with unusually small ionic size, distinctive bonding behavior, and outsized importance in electrochemical technology.
Lightest metal
[He] 2s1 valence structure
Commonly forms Li+
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
4 Be
Beryllium
4
Beryllium is a light, stiff alkaline earth metal whose very small doubly charged ion makes its chemistry far more covalent and hazardous than group trends alone would suggest.
[He] 2s2 valence structure
Small, highly polarizing Be2+
High stiffness-to-weight ratio
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
5 B
Boron
5
Boron is a metalloid whose electron deficiency, covalent networking, and unusual cluster chemistry make it one of the most conceptually distinctive light elements.
Lightest group 13 element
Electron-deficient covalent chemistry
Important isotopes B-10 and B-11
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
6 C
Carbon
6
Carbon is a period-2 nonmetal whose ability to catenate and hybridize makes it the structural core of organic chemistry, major industrial materials, and the global carbon cycle.
Tetravalent p-block element
Supports sp, sp2, and sp3 bonding
Many allotropes from diamond to graphene
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
7 N
Nitrogen
7
Nitrogen is a pnictogen whose elemental stability as N2 contrasts with the extreme chemical importance of its fixed compounds in biology, agriculture, and atmospheric chemistry.
Half-filled 2p subshell
Very strong N≡N bond
78% of Earth’s atmosphere
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
8 O
Oxygen
8
Oxygen is a strongly electronegative chalcogen whose bonding power shapes minerals, water, respiration, combustion, and the chemistry of the atmosphere.
Highly electronegative chalcogen
Paramagnetic O2 molecule
Major component of air and water
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
9 F
Fluorine
9
Fluorine is the most electronegative element and the most aggressive common elemental oxidant, giving it a chemistry dominated by strong fluoride bond formation and severe handling challenges.
Most electronegative element
Powerful elemental oxidant
Forms very strong M-F and C-F bonds
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
10 Ne
Neon
10
Neon is a noble gas with a filled second shell, famous for its emission colors and important as a model of inert atomic behavior in lighting, lasers, and gas-separation technology.
Closed-shell noble gas
Red-orange glow in discharge tubes
Recovered from liquid air
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
11 Na
Sodium
11
Sodium is a soft alkali metal whose dominant Na+ ion underlies salts, aqueous electrolytes, chemical reductions, and large-scale inorganic industry.
Classic alkali metal
Na+ dominates saline waters
Soft metal stored under oil
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
12 Mg
Magnesium
12
Magnesium is a light alkaline-earth metal whose stable Mg2+ ion and low density make it central to alloys, minerals, organometallic reagents, and biology.
Light structural metal
Mg2+ stabilizes ATP chemistry
Burns with brilliant white light
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
13 Al
Aluminum
13
Aluminum is a light, abundant metal whose stable oxide film, high specific strength, and Al3+ chemistry make it central to materials, packaging, and industrial electrochemistry.
Lightweight structural metal
Protected by oxide passivation
Produced by Hall-Heroult electrolysis
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
14 Si
Silicon
14
Silicon is a covalent metalloid whose network structures, oxide chemistry, and semiconducting behavior make it central to minerals, electronics, and modern materials.
Metalloid and semiconductor
Second-most abundant crustal element
Forms silica and silicates
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
15 P
Phosphorus
15
Phosphorus is a reactive nonmetal whose allotropes, oxoacids, phosphates, and reduced compounds make it central to fertilizers, biochemistry, and energetic chemistry.
Pnictogen nonmetal
Forms white, red, and black allotropes
Essential in DNA and ATP chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
16 S
Sulfur
16
Sulfur is a chalcogen nonmetal whose allotropy, sulfide and sulfate chemistry, and huge role in sulfuric acid production make it foundational to industry and biogeochemistry.
Yellow chalcogen solid
Common as S8 rings
Central to sulfuric acid
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
17 Cl
Chlorine
17
Chlorine is a highly reactive halogen whose oxidizing power, chloride chemistry, and disinfectant applications make it central to industrial, environmental, and biological chemistry.
Reactive halogen gas
Strong oxidizing agent
Produced by chlor-alkali electrolysis
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
18 Ar
Argon
18
Argon is a chemically inert noble gas recovered from air and valued for providing nonreactive atmospheres in science, metallurgy, and lighting.
Noble gas from air
Most abundant atmospheric noble gas
Used for inert atmospheres
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
19 K
Potassium
19
Potassium is a highly reactive alkali metal whose K+ ion is essential in biology and agriculture, while the metal itself illustrates the extreme reducing behavior of group-1 chemistry.
Very reactive alkali metal
K+ is a major intracellular ion
Key nutrient in fertilizers
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
20 Ca
Calcium
20
Calcium is an abundant alkaline-earth metal whose Ca2+ ion dominates mineral carbonates, biological hard tissues, and many industrial inorganic materials.
Alkaline earth metal
Major component of limestone
Essential in bones and shells
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
21 Sc
Scandium
21
Scandium is an early transition metal whose strongly favored Sc3+ chemistry and scarcity make it important in specialty alloys, ceramics, and separation chemistry rather than in bulk metallurgy.
Early transition metal
Sc3+ dominates its chemistry
Added to advanced aluminum alloys
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
22 Ti
Titanium
22
Titanium is a strong, low-density transition metal whose oxide chemistry, corrosion resistance, and biocompatibility make it central to pigments, aerospace alloys, and implants.
Strong low-density metal
Protected by TiO2 passivation
Derived from ilmenite and rutile
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
23 V
Vanadium
23
Vanadium is a transition metal notable for broad oxidation-state chemistry, catalytic oxides, and alloying value in steels and emerging electrochemical technologies.
Multiple accessible oxidation states
Important steel alloy addition
V2O5 is a major catalyst
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
24 Cr
Chromium
24
Chromium is a hard transition metal whose passivating oxide, colorful coordination chemistry, and high-oxidation-state oxoanions make it essential in alloys, plating, and redox chemistry.
Hard lustrous metal
Passivates stainless steel
Chromate chemistry is strongly oxidizing
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
25 Mn
Manganese
25
Manganese is a transition metal whose oxidation-state diversity makes it essential to steelmaking, batteries, oxidants, and biological redox chemistry.
Important steelmaking additive
Broad redox chemistry
MnO2 is a battery material
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
26 Fe
Iron
26
Iron is a foundational transition metal whose abundance, redox chemistry, magnetism, and central role in steelmaking make it one of the most important elements in technology and geology.
Core metal of steelmaking
Common as Fe2+ and Fe3+
Abundant in Earth and industry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
27 Co
Cobalt
27
Cobalt is a transition metal known for coordination chemistry, magnetic alloys, battery materials, and the long-lived radioisotope cobalt-60.
Transition metal with rich coordination chemistry
Important in battery cathodes
Used in superalloys and magnets
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
28 Ni
Nickel
28
Nickel is a corrosion-resistant transition metal central to stainless alloys, catalysis, electroplating, and modern battery materials.
Corrosion-resistant alloy metal
Common as Ni2+ in chemistry
Important catalyst and battery material
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
29 Cu
Copper
29
Copper is a highly conductive transition metal whose redox chemistry, corrosion products, and alloy behavior make it indispensable in wiring, architecture, and catalysis.
Excellent electrical conductor
Reddish metallic luster
Common as Cu+ and Cu2+
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
30 Zn
Zinc
30
Zinc is a d-block metal whose Zn2+ chemistry, sacrificial-corrosion behavior, and large-scale galvanizing role make it central to protective coatings, alloys, and bioinorganic chemistry.
Commonly forms Zn2+
Used for galvanizing steel
Important in brass and die casting
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
31 Ga
Gallium
31
Gallium is a low-melting post-transition metal whose trivalent chemistry and semiconductor compounds make it important in optoelectronics, power devices, and specialty alloys.
Melts near room temperature
Ga3+ chemistry dominates
Key semiconductor metal
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
32 Ge
Germanium
32
Germanium is a metalloid whose semiconductor behavior and infrared-optical uses make it important in electronics, optics, and specialty materials.
Classic semiconductor metalloid
Important infrared material
Ge4+ chemistry is common
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
33 As
Arsenic
33
Arsenic is a toxic group-15 metalloid whose oxidation-state chemistry, mineral occurrence, and semiconductor compounds make it important in environmental science and materials chemistry.
Toxic metalloid
As3+ and As5+ are environmentally important
Common in sulfide minerals
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
34 Se
Selenium
34
Selenium is a chalcogen nonmetal known for allotropy, photoconductive behavior, trace nutritional importance, and toxicity at elevated exposure.
Chalcogen with several allotropes
Photoconductive element
Trace nutrient in biology
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
35 Br
Bromine
35
Bromine is a volatile halogen notable as the only common liquid nonmetal, with rich redox chemistry spanning bromide salts, oxidants, and organobromine compounds.
Only common liquid nonmetal
Recovered from brines
Forms bromide and oxidized bromine species
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
36 Kr
Krypton
36
Krypton is a noble gas recovered from air whose inertness, emission behavior, and rare compound chemistry make it useful in lighting, lasers, and spectroscopy.
Trace atmospheric noble gas
Recovered from liquid air
Bright discharge emission
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
37 Rb
Rubidium
37
Rubidium is a very reactive heavy alkali metal whose Rb+ chemistry is simpler than its elemental reactivity, making it more important in precision physics than in bulk industry.
Heavy alkali metal
Forms Rb+ salts
Important in atomic clocks
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
38 Sr
Strontium
38
Strontium is an alkaline-earth metal best known for Sr2+ salts, bright crimson flame colors, and the environmental and medical significance of selected isotopes.
Alkaline earth metal
Forms Sr2+ salts
Crimson flame color
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
39 Y
Yttrium
39
Yttrium is a group-3 metal with strongly favored Y3+ chemistry, widely used in phosphors, ceramics, lasers, and high-temperature oxide materials.
Group-3 metal
Y3+ dominates chemistry
Important in phosphors
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
40 Zr
Zirconium
40
Zirconium is a corrosion-resistant transition metal whose oxide stability, nuclear applications, and refractory ceramics make it important in engineering and high-temperature materials.
Corrosion-resistant metal
Zr4+ chemistry dominates
Important in reactor cladding
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
41 Nb
Niobium
41
Niobium is a transition metal valued for ferroniobium alloying, superconducting materials, and oxide or halide chemistry centered on the +5 state.
Refractory transition metal
Nb5+ chemistry is common
Major steel alloy addition
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
42 Mo
Molybdenum
42
Molybdenum is a refractory transition metal notable for strong Mo=O chemistry, catalytic oxides and sulfides, alloying value, and important radioisotopes in medicine.
Refractory transition metal
Multiple oxidation states
MoO3 and MoS2 are major materials
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
43 Tc
Technetium
43
Technetium is the lightest element with no stable isotopes and is best known through pertechnetate chemistry and the diagnostic-radioisotope role of technetium-99m.
No stable isotopes
Pertechnetate chemistry is central
Tc-99m is medically indispensable
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
44 Ru
Ruthenium
44
Ruthenium is a platinum-group transition metal known for robust catalytic chemistry, multiple oxidation states, and specialized electronic or alloy uses.
Platinum-group metal
Multiple oxidation states
Important catalyst metal
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
45 Rh
Rhodium
45
Rhodium is a rare platinum-group metal prized for catalytic selectivity, chemical durability, and reflective plating applications.
Rare platinum-group metal
Rhodium catalysts are highly selective
Corrosion resistant
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
46 Pd
Palladium
46
Palladium is a platinum-group metal whose surface chemistry, hydrogen affinity, and catalytic versatility make it central to synthesis, emissions control, and electronics.
Platinum-group metal
Excellent catalyst metal
Absorbs hydrogen
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
47 Ag
Silver
47
Silver is a noble coinage metal celebrated for conductivity, reflectivity, and rich Ag+ chemistry in photography, antimicrobial materials, and electronics.
Coinage metal
Highest electrical conductivity
Ag+ chemistry is central
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
48 Cd
Cadmium
48
Cadmium is a soft d-block metal whose Cd2+ chemistry, historical pigments, battery uses, and serious toxicity make it chemically useful but tightly regulated.
Recovered with zinc
Cd2+ chemistry dominates
Important in CdTe and NiCd history
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
49 In
Indium
49
Indium is a soft post-transition metal whose low melting point and transparent-conductor compounds make it central to display technology, solders, and semiconductor materials.
Very soft metal
In3+ chemistry dominates
Important in ITO coatings
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
50 Sn
Tin
50
Tin is a soft post-transition metal whose two common oxidation states, useful alloys, and corrosion-resistant coatings make it technologically important far beyond its simple appearance.
Post-transition metal
Sn2+ and Sn4+ are common
Important in solders and tinplate
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
51 Sb
Antimony
51
Antimony is a metalloid whose Sb(III) and Sb(V) chemistry supports flame-retardant systems, alloys, semiconductors, and important toxicological studies.
Metalloid pnictogen
Sb3+ and Sb5+ are common
Used in flame-retardant systems
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
52 Te
Tellurium
52
Tellurium is a rare chalcogen metalloid whose alloying, thermoelectric, and photovoltaic roles make it important despite low abundance and byproduct supply.
Rare chalcogen metalloid
Recovered as a byproduct
Important in thermoelectrics and CdTe
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
53 I
Iodine
53
Iodine is a heavy halogen whose iodide chemistry, biological thyroid role, and oxidized iodine compounds make it important in medicine, nutrition, and synthesis.
Heavy halogen
Iodide is biologically essential
Sublimes to violet vapor
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
54 Xe
Xenon
54
Xenon is a heavy noble gas whose rare but real compound chemistry, brilliant discharge behavior, and specialized medical or propulsion uses distinguish it from lighter noble gases.
Heavy noble gas
Forms rare fluorides
Important in lamps and ion thrusters
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
55 Cs
Cesium
55
Cesium is a very heavy alkali metal whose extreme reactivity contrasts with the extraordinary precision of cesium-based frequency standards and atomic measurements.
Heaviest stable alkali metal
Cs+ dominates chemistry
Defines the SI second
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
56 Ba
Barium
56
Barium is a heavy alkaline-earth metal whose Ba2+ salts are important in drilling, pyrotechnics, medical imaging, and selected electronic ceramics.
Heavy alkaline earth metal
Ba2+ dominates chemistry
Barite is the main ore
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
57 La
Lanthanum
57
Lanthanum is a rare earth metal whose La3+ chemistry, oxide basicity, and catalytic or optical applications make it an important entry point to the lanthanide series.
Rare earth metal
La3+ dominates chemistry
Recovered with lanthanides
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
58 Ce
Cerium
58
Cerium is a lanthanide notable for accessible Ce3+ and Ce4+ chemistry, oxygen-storage behavior, and widespread use in catalysts, polishing powders, and alloys.
Lanthanide with Ce3+/Ce4+ chemistry
Important in catalysts
Used in glass polishing
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
59 Pr
Praseodymium
59
Praseodymium is a lanthanide whose Pr3+ chemistry dominates, but whose optical, magnetic, and occasional Pr4+ behavior make it useful in glasses, magnets, and ceramics.
Lanthanide metal
Pr3+ dominates chemistry
Used in magnets and glasses
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
60 Nd
Neodymium
60
Neodymium is a lanthanide best known for strong Nd3+ chemistry, intense optical transitions, and its central role in the highest-performance permanent magnets.
Lanthanide metal
Nd3+ dominates chemistry
Key to Nd-Fe-B magnets
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
61 Pm
Promethium
61
Promethium is a radioactive lanthanide with no stable isotopes, known mainly through nuclear production, tracer uses, and historical luminous or power applications.
No stable isotopes
Lanthanide metal
Pm3+ dominates chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
62 Sm
Samarium
62
Samarium is a lanthanide whose Sm3+ chemistry dominates, while Sm2+ and selected isotopes make it important in magnets, absorbers, and geochronology.
Lanthanide metal
Sm3+ dominates chemistry
Used in Sm-Co magnets
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
63 Eu
Europium
63
Europium is a lanthanide famous for intense luminescence and for the unusual accessibility of both Eu2+ and Eu3+ chemistry in phosphors and advanced optical materials.
Lanthanide phosphor element
Eu2+ and Eu3+ are both important
Strong red and blue luminescence
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
64 Gd
Gadolinium
64
Gadolinium is a lanthanide distinguished by strong magnetism, useful Gd3+ chemistry, and exceptional neutron-absorption behavior that supports medical and nuclear technologies.
Lanthanide metal
Gd3+ dominates chemistry
Strong magnetic behavior
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
65 Tb
Terbium
65
Terbium is a lanthanide whose Tb3+ chemistry and green luminescence make it valuable in phosphors, magnetostrictive materials, and specialized optical systems.
Lanthanide metal
Tb3+ dominates chemistry
Strong green phosphor ion
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
66 Dy
Dysprosium
66
Dysprosium is a lanthanide valued for strong magnetic behavior, neutron absorption, and its role in improving high-temperature permanent magnets.
Lanthanide metal
Dy3+ dominates chemistry
Used in high-temperature magnets
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
67 Ho
Holmium
67
Holmium is a lanthanide known for very high magnetic moment, Ho3+ chemistry, and specialized uses in lasers, magnets, and neutron-related materials.
Lanthanide metal
Ho3+ dominates chemistry
Very high magnetic moment
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
68 Er
Erbium
68
Erbium is a lanthanide whose Er3+ optical transitions make it indispensable in fiber-optic amplification, lasers, and specialty glass coloration.
Lanthanide metal
Er3+ dominates chemistry
Critical in fiber amplifiers
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
69 Tm
Thulium
69
Thulium is a scarce lanthanide whose Tm3+ chemistry and specialized optical transitions support niche lasers, portable X-ray sources, and research materials.
Scarce lanthanide
Tm3+ dominates chemistry
Specialized laser ion
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
70 Yb
Ytterbium
70
Ytterbium is a lanthanide whose closed 4f shell makes Yb2+ unusually accessible and whose Yb3+ ions are important in lasers, clocks, and optical materials.
Late lanthanide metal
Yb2+ and Yb3+ both matter
Important in lasers and clocks
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
71 Lu
Lutetium
71
Lutetium is the final lanthanide, valued for stable Lu3+ chemistry, dense oxide materials, and the medical and detector importance of selected isotopes and compounds.
Final lanthanide
Lu3+ dominates chemistry
Important in scintillators
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
72 Hf
Hafnium
72
Hafnium is a dense refractory transition metal whose Hf4+ chemistry, oxide stability, and neutron absorption make it important in nuclear and microelectronic materials.
Refractory transition metal
Hf4+ dominates chemistry
Strong neutron absorber
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
73 Ta
Tantalum
73
Tantalum is a refractory transition metal prized for chemical resistance, stable oxide films, capacitor technology, and high-performance alloys.
Refractory transition metal
Ta5+ chemistry dominates
Highly corrosion resistant
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
74 W
Tungsten
74
Tungsten is a refractory transition metal with the highest melting point of any pure metal, important in hard materials, high-temperature components, and tungsten(VI) oxide chemistry.
Highest-melting pure metal
Refractory transition metal
Important in carbides and alloys
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
75 Re
Rhenium
75
Rhenium is a dense refractory transition metal whose wide oxidation-state chemistry and high-temperature durability make it important in superalloys and catalysis.
Refractory transition metal
Broad oxidation-state chemistry
Important in superalloys
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
76 Os
Osmium
76
Osmium is a dense platinum-group metal whose noble-metal bulk behavior contrasts sharply with the volatility and toxicity of osmium tetroxide.
Densest naturally occurring element
Platinum-group metal
OsO4 is a defining compound
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
77 Ir
Iridium
77
Iridium is a very dense noble metal valued for exceptional corrosion resistance, high-temperature stability, and specialized catalytic or electrochemical roles.
Very dense noble metal
Highly corrosion resistant
Platinum-group element
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
78 Pt
Platinum
78
Platinum is a noble transition metal central to catalysis, electrochemistry, jewelry, and high-value chemical processing because of its balance of reactivity and durability.
Noble transition metal
Premier catalyst metal
Highly corrosion resistant
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
79 Au
Gold
79
Gold is a noble coinage metal famous for chemical inertness, conductivity, and surface stability, yet still capable of rich Au(I) and Au(III) coordination chemistry.
Noble coinage metal
Native gold occurs
Au(I) and Au(III) chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
80 Hg
Mercury
80
Mercury is the only metal liquid at room temperature, with unusual Hg(I) and Hg(II) chemistry that is scientifically important but tightly governed by toxicity concerns.
Liquid metal at room temperature
Hg(I) and Hg(II) chemistry
Forms amalgams
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
81 Tl
Thallium
81
Thallium is a heavy post-transition metal whose Tl(I) chemistry dominates because of the inert-pair effect, and whose toxicology has greatly narrowed its acceptable uses.
Heavy group-13 metal
Tl(I) is strongly favored
Inert-pair effect is important
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
82 Pb
Lead
82
Lead is a dense post-transition metal whose Pb(II) chemistry, corrosion products, and historical ubiquity in pipes, pigments, and batteries are now viewed through major public-health concerns.
Dense post-transition metal
Pb(II) dominates chemistry
Central to lead-acid batteries
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
83 Bi
Bismuth
83
Bismuth is a heavy p-block metal whose Bi(III) chemistry, low-toxicity profile relative to lead, and low-melting alloys make it valuable in medicines, alloys, and oxide materials.
Heavy pnictogen metal
Bi(III) dominates chemistry
Low-melting alloy component
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
84 Po
Polonium
84
Polonium is a highly radioactive heavy chalcogen whose ordinary chemistry is overshadowed by severe radiological hazard and its historical place in nuclear science.
Highly radioactive element
Heavy chalcogen
Known mainly in nuclear science
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
85 At
Astatine
85
Astatine is a very rare radioactive halogen whose chemistry blends halogen behavior with unusually metallic and radiochemical character.
Radioactive halogen
Very scarce in nature
Shows mixed halogen and metallic behavior
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
86 Rn
Radon
86
Radon is a radioactive noble gas whose inert closed-shell chemistry is overshadowed by its importance in indoor-air exposure and radiation health.
Radioactive noble gas
Member of uranium decay chains
Indoor-air health hazard
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
87 Fr
Francium
87
Francium is an extremely rare radioactive alkali metal whose chemistry is inferred mostly from periodic trends, tracer studies, and atomic-physics experiments.
Heaviest alkali metal
Extremely rare
Known mainly from tracer and atomic studies
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
88 Ra
Radium
88
Radium is a radioactive alkaline-earth metal whose Ra2+ chemistry resembles barium but whose historical and radiological significance is far greater.
Radioactive alkaline-earth metal
Ra2+ dominates chemistry
Historic role in radiochemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
89 Ac
Actinium
89
Actinium is an early actinide whose Ac3+ chemistry resembles lanthanum, but whose radioactivity makes it scientifically important in nuclear medicine and radiochemistry.
Early actinide metal
Ac3+ dominates chemistry
Strong alpha-emitter isotopes
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
90 Th
Thorium
90
Thorium is an abundant actinide whose strongly tetravalent chemistry underlies refractory oxide materials, fertile-fuel concepts, and classic actinide coordination chemistry.
Abundant actinide
Th4+ dominates chemistry
ThO2 is highly refractory
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
91 Pa
Protactinium
91
Protactinium is a scarce radioactive actinide whose Pa(V) chemistry provides an important bridge between thorium and uranium behavior.
Scarce actinide
Pa(V) is central
Bridge between Th and U chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
92 U
Uranium
92
Uranium is the most consequential actinide in energy and geochemistry, with multivalent chemistry built around U(IV) solids and the uranyl U(VI) ion.
Central nuclear-fuel element
Uranyl chemistry is major
Occurs in uranium minerals
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
93 Np
Neptunium
93
Neptunium is the first transuranium element and a multivalent actinide whose Np(V) and Np(VI) chemistry is important in fuel-cycle separations and long-term waste behavior.
First transuranium element
Wide oxidation-state range
Important in fuel-cycle chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
94 Pu
Plutonium
94
Plutonium is a central transuranium element whose complex allotropy, redox flexibility, and isotope-specific behavior dominate nuclear fuel, weapons history, and waste management.
Major fissile element
Multiple oxidation states
Complex allotropy
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
95 Am
Americium
95
Americium is a transuranium actinide best known for Am(III) chemistry, smoke-detector sources, and its role in advanced waste and separations research.
Am(III) chemistry is common
Used in smoke detectors
Transuranium actinide

0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
96 Cm
Curium
96
Curium is a strongly radioactive actinide whose Cm(III) chemistry and intense alpha emission matter in research, source materials, and actinide science.
Strongly radioactive actinide
Cm3+ dominates chemistry
Important research target material
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
97 Bk
Berkelium
97
Berkelium is a synthetic actinide whose Bk(III) and Bk(IV) chemistry is important mainly for target preparation and research on heavier elements.
Synthetic actinide
Bk3+ dominates with accessible Bk4+
Used as target material
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
98 Cf
Californium
98
Californium is a synthetic actinide best known for Cf-252, an intense neutron emitter used in specialized industrial and scientific applications.
Synthetic actinide
Cf-252 is a strong neutron source
Mostly Cf3+ chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
99 Es
Einsteinium
99
Einsteinium is a highly radioactive synthetic actinide used mainly in fundamental chemistry and as a precursor for making heavier elements.
Synthetic late actinide
Es3+ dominates chemistry
Used in heavy-element research
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
100 Fm
Fermium
100
Fermium is a very heavy synthetic actinide whose chemistry is known only through atom-scale or microgram-scale studies.
Very heavy actinide
Known only in tiny amounts
Mostly Fm3+ chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
101 Md
Mendelevium
101
Mendelevium is a synthetic late actinide whose chemistry is known from atom-scale experiments and notable Md(II)/Md(III) behavior.
Synthetic late actinide
Md2+ and Md3+ are relevant
Atom-scale chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
102 No
Nobelium
102
Nobelium is a late actinide notable for comparatively stable No(II) chemistry, which distinguishes it from many earlier actinides.
Filled 5f shell
No2+ is relatively stable
Late actinide
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
103 Lr
Lawrencium
103
Lawrencium is the last actinide and a synthetic heavy element whose chemistry closes the 5f series while raising questions about the f-block to d-block transition.
Last actinide
Synthetic element
Lr3+ dominates chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
104 Rf
Rutherfordium
104
Rutherfordium is the first transactinide element, expected to behave as a group-4 metal with chemistry analogous to hafnium and zirconium.
First transactinide
Group-4 analog
Known from atom-at-a-time chemistry
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
105 Db
Dubnium
105
Dubnium is a synthetic group-5 element whose chemistry is studied through volatile halides and oxyhalides that connect it to niobium and tantalum.
Synthetic group-5 element
Db(V) is the key state
Studied through halides and oxyhalides
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
106 Sg
Seaborgium
106
Seaborgium is a synthetic group-6 element expected to resemble tungsten and molybdenum through high-valent oxide, halide, and carbonyl chemistry.
Synthetic group-6 element
High-valent chemistry is central
Compared with tungsten
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
107 Bh
Bohrium
107
Bohrium is a synthetic group-7 element expected to continue rhenium and technetium chemistry into the superheavy region.
Synthetic group-7 element
High oxidation states expected
Compared with rhenium
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
108 Hs
Hassium
108
Hassium is a synthetic group-8 element notable because hassium tetroxide chemistry provides a rare direct comparison with osmium.
Synthetic group-8 element
HsO4 chemistry is important
Compared with osmium
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
109 Mt
Meitnerium
109
Meitnerium is a synthetic group-9 element whose chemistry is largely predicted from relativistic calculations and trends from iridium and rhodium.
Synthetic group-9 element
Chemistry mostly predicted
Compared with iridium
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
110 Ds
Darmstadtium
110
Darmstadtium is a synthetic group-10 element expected to show noble-metal chemistry related to platinum and palladium, though experiments remain very limited.
Synthetic group-10 element
Noble-metal behavior expected
Compared with platinum
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
111 Rg
Roentgenium
111
Roentgenium is a synthetic group-11 element expected to continue coinage-metal trends with strong relativistic modification.
Synthetic coinage-metal analogue
Relativistic effects are strong
Compared with gold
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
112 Cn
Copernicium
112
Copernicium is a synthetic group-12 element whose closed-shell-like electronic structure may make it unusually volatile and weakly metallic.
Synthetic group-12 element
Relativistic stabilization is strong
Possibly unusually volatile
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
113 Nh
Nihonium
113
Nihonium is a synthetic heavy p-block element expected to show strong inert-pair effects and chemistry closer to Tl(I) than to lighter group-13 elements.
Synthetic group-13 element
Strong inert-pair effect expected
Nh(I) may be important
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
114 Fl
Flerovium
114
Flerovium is a synthetic group-14 element predicted to show very strong inert-pair effects and unexpectedly weak metallic or volatile behavior.
Synthetic group-14 element
Strong inert-pair effect expected
May be unusually volatile
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
115 Mc
Moscovium
115
Moscovium is a synthetic heavy pnictogen expected to display strong inert-pair effects and chemistry more metallic than bismuth.
Synthetic group-15 element
Heavy pnictogen behavior
Strong inert-pair effect expected
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
116 Lv
Livermorium
116
Livermorium is a synthetic heavy chalcogen expected to show strong relativistic stabilization and oxidation-state preferences unlike sulfur or selenium.
Synthetic heavy chalcogen
Low oxidation states may be favored
Chemistry mostly theoretical
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
117 Ts
Tennessine
117
Tennessine is a synthetic superheavy halogen whose chemistry may be much less purely halogen-like than chlorine, bromine, or iodine.
Synthetic halogen
Heavy-element effects are extreme
Halogen behavior may be weakened
Progress
0%
0 of 8 chapters complete
118 Og
Oganesson
118
Oganesson is the heaviest known element and a superheavy group-18 element predicted to be far less idealized as a noble gas than helium through radon.
Heaviest known element
Group-18 member
May not behave like a simple noble gas

0 of 8 chapters complete
